TOP>>

Latency

Loop Type Latency Latency Index State No. Folding Loop Folding Stages Total Folding States Hazard User Operator Sub Loops Line
Total S 1 + L1 4 1, 2, 3, 4 - - - - - L1 computer.cpp:263
L1 S 3 * N1 + {1, 3} 3 2, 3, 4 - - - - - - computer.cpp:266
  • Loop: loop name (L#)
  • Type: loop type ([S]equential/[F]olding)
  • Latency: latency of loop
    • Reset: reset state
    • L#: latency of loop L#
    • N#: number of iteration (not necessarily same as number of iteration in description)
  • Latency Index: Index to measure latency. (A relative index for the same description. Smaller value means smaller number of cycles between input and output.)
  • State No.: state numbers included in loop
  • Folding Loop: folding loop name (FL#)
  • Folding Stages: number of stages in folding loop
  • Total Folding States: number of states in folding loop (inner loops included)
  • Hazard: existence of hazard in folding loop (Exist/None)
  • User Operator: user operators used in loop
  • Sub Loops: inner loop

Critical path delay can be larger than clock constraint because
false paths are not excluded in timing report.
Static timing analyzer takes a conservative approach.
The logic synthesis tool usually reduces the critical path delay to meet the timing constraint.

TOP>>

Terminology

(*1) Combinational Loop
See *.err file for detailed loop information.
(*2) Total Net Count
Count one siterminal net is counted as one.
The number of fanouts is not counted.
Nets inside RTL parts such as functional units, multmultiplexers, and decoders,
are included when BLIB version 2.00 or later is usedused.
(*3) Total Pin Pair Count
Count one fanout as 1 for each signal.
Pin pairs inside RTL parts such as functional unitunits, multiplexers, and decoders
are included when BLIB version 2.00 or later is usedused.
(*4) Net Count
Nets inside RTL parts such as functional units, multmultiplexers, and decoders are not included.
(*5) Pin Pair Count
Pin pairs inside RTL parts such as functional unitunits, multiplexers, and decoders are not included.
(*6) Total
The number of all pin pairs counted by every bit for a signal.
(*7) Max
Maximum value among fanouts counted by every bit for for a signal.
The number in ( ) represents the number of bits withwith the maximum fanout.

TOP>>
